g.text: STARTING POINT u2014 Orders service calls Customers service via REST
2. CI on Orders runs the test against a STUB of Customers
3. PRODUCER's CI verifies it satisfies the contract
4. Producer wants to change something (e.g. remove 'email' field)

text.tspan: Orders team writes a CONTRACT TEST (using Pact or Spring Cloud Contract):
'When I GET /customers/42,
I expect status 200 and body matching:
{ id: number, name: string, email: string, address: { ... } }'
Generates a CONTRACT FILE (JSON describing the expectation).
Uploads to a contract broker (Pact Broker, Git repo, S3).
Pact spins up a local stub that satisfies the contract.
Orders code calls the stub u2192 verifies its own behavior.
u2192 Orders is confident: 'if Customers honors this contract, I work.'
When Customers CI runs:
pull all contracts from the broker that mention Customers.
For each: replay the request against the REAL Customers service.
If response matches the contract u2192 PASS
If not u2192 BREAKING CHANGE; FAIL THE BUILD
u2192 Producer can't ship a breaking change without consumer's knowledge.
Producer changes field; CI fails because Orders' contract still expects email.
u2192 Producer KNOWS the change breaks Orders.
Options:
u2022 talk to Orders team u2014 do they still need it?
u2022 deprecate via versioning (add /v2 endpoint without email)
u2022 wait for Orders to update their contract first
u2192 NO surprise breakages in production.
GET /customers/{id}
u2192 { id, name, email, address }
